---

##    21\. Secondary Effects 

The reason our culture is f-cked right now is because most people are afraid of sticking to their guns.

And who can blame them? 

We're only rewarded for instant gratification.

People don't double-down on themselves because of the silly fear of being an outcast.

So they stay within the lines. 

Making themselves... Invisible. 

But the truth is... If you're doing something you know is right... Or that's new... And could moving our society forward... And if you can take the criticism for a while... Then you need to hang in there.

Eventually, you'll be celebrated. Or at least you'll find your tribe of people to connect with.

But you have to keep the smoke going long enough to be rescued.

Yes, every action. Has a reaction.

But every reaction...

Has a secondary effect.

And the totally weird thing about it is that the secondary effect is usually polar opposite to the reaction.

If the reaction was negative. The secondary effect is positive. And vice versa. How crazy is that math?

The most basic example is going to the gym.

If you kill yourself on legs day, well, afterwards you can hardly walk.

Sometimes you're slow for days.

But what happens after a few months?

You start to become an athlete.

See how that works?

But nobody talks about the secondary effects. 

They talk about "don't do that, it'll hurt".

And everyone listens. Except a few. The few who know about secondary effects.

Now YOU know about it to.

Once you understand it you can shut out the noise.

But remember. If you're doing something different than the norm, you have to be patient with the crowd.

But they'll understand it eventually.

I've been there. Over and over again. It's almost "yawn" at this point because it's so predictable.

I'll do something crazy. People get mad. And then a year later they hire me for thing they used to get mad at me for.

That's why I encourage you to go out on a limb.

Double down on yourself. Double down on the things that make you different.

Too many artists aren’t sticking around long enough to reap the benefits of secondary effect

The things may be shocking tay...

_In a year that's what they'll be hiring YOU for.  _

[If you want to go further down the rabbit hole READ THIS.](https://www.inc.com/julian-hayes-ii/ray-dalios-technique-to-instantly-becoming-a-better-decision-maker-boils-down-to-8-words.html)

The way I needed this! You really don't think about the secondary effect. OR, you don't wait long enough for it. That's where I fit in...not waiting long enough for it. DOUBLE THE F*^! DOWN LENNY!

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/12084289/comments/27435928)

So crazy how that works. I've experienced that at work or in my prior jobs a lot. My "Boss" would say things like, "that's not realistic", or "it's unproven", or you're messing with "established things" and you're gonna get yourself, me, or us in "trouble". Only problem with me is that I would get so frustrated that I would leave after a while; and then a year or more later, the #ass who said these things to me is now running this cool new "initiative" or "idea" that I came up with a year or years prior. FML. I'm doing things differently with my art.

Cashing in for short-term pleasure leads to long-term agony.*This quote was in the Ray Dalio piece and it really got in my head! This was a hard module for me it really put the mirror in your face and made you realize you have to make decisions like this if you truly want to be successful and see art all the way through.

![Steven Rivera](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/31a5c4502112f7dee513d903842a3538?d=mm&s=70)

Steven Rivera

Awaiting Review 

3 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/12084289/comments/20614248)

This one caught me in sync! Just put out the most vulnerable podcast episode I've done. It felt like a stream of consciousness as I was doing it, I was in such a flow. BUT, I have been cringing all day and doing my best not to listen back. I don't have much of a following but everything I've posted = crickets. I've been thinking a lot about the idea of just saying fuck it and dropping bombs. I've also been thinking about another podcast clip I saw where the guy was talking about how long form content, especially Youtube/Podcasts have a cumulative effect, where the results continually appreciate over time. This video strikes the same chord on a much deeper level. I'm so thankful for finding this community, having an extremely limited amount of creative folks around me. This school gives me the JUICE

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

As I wrote to you in an email today... Recording a podcast and promoting the podcast go hand in hand. You should know how you will promote it before you even record a word. That will keep you honest. Only then is the effect cummulative.

NEUEREALITAET

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/12084289/comments/20036674)

Secondary consequences and long-term thinking, sticking to your goals and values: I am so in line with this. It feels so much better and more true to me to live that way. It gives me a sense of calm. I feel like the outside world constantly tries to push me into being short-term and deviate from my goals. So it is great to find this lesson here because it reinforces my natural tendency and does not make me feel wrong about it. Thank you!

![Greg Fender](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/5b685a801a5dc3223285988e9cfbffd0?d=mm&s=70)

Greg Fender

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/12084289/comments/17889628)

Here’s some more of that synchronicity! Since I’ve been doing my daily practice, not only has my art become easier, but I’ve somehow gained the ability to improvise over all kinds of music on my guitar. I’ve never been a music theory guy and I always just played and wrote music “by ear.” I’m still finding my way and I have a super long way to go, but being able to improvise is so incredibly powerful and will help my art so much that I feel like that secondary effect alone has been worth the daily practice. I’m going to keep this train going and see what other things I can uncover. If I’ve come to this after only 24 days I can’t imagine what 5 or 10 years will bring.

NOw you're talking. The key is to keep the intensity of your pursuit up-- never let it become routine. That's how the intensity of rewards will keep flowing your way.

![Melvin Todd](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/b63b6c7e71eb3338a6757559c6f8f360?d=mm&s=70)

Melvin Todd

Awaiting Review 

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/12084289/comments/14622858)

This hit extra hard. Since I've been posting my daily practice on the socials, sometime the numbers fluctuate like crazy. In the past that would have been enough for me to stop, but now, I don't let it bother me much because I know I'm putting my all in it and dropping bombs in-between my atomic bombs. I am cognizant that every drawing is making me better. But Then I look, and new people are finding all of this art, and something magical is happening. They trust that I'm consistent and wanting more. It's only been a month, but I'm already picturing how big this snowball gets in a year...two, five, ten. Secondary effects. That's what I'll think about now as I create.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

![Emmanuele Bettari](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/dec2584fd5c6dafa727bd447da3df90f?d=mm&s=70)

Emmanuele Bettari

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/12084289/comments/13675061)

One of my Post-its on my bedroom wall: "Your own path". It means to make sure I'm not doing things to homologate myself. I'm not creating to feel safe. I'm creating to feel that I'm finding something mine. Deeply mine. That maybe I want to share. Is slower than the most popular? Is faster? Doesn't matter. It's my own, and I won't stop here. Looking for secondary effects.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

Ashris Choudhury

Most people who drift through life are not looking at the secondary effect. Short-term comfort over long-term success. Gotta push through leg day. It's easy for me to say this tonight, the day before Day 1, but all of this is hitting home and giving me a real reason to start this 21-days again, but within these ideas. These ideas are hitting home for me. Let's do this.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

I feel that this is for me. Most of the time i just go back to common sense because it's confortable, I feel accepted, but not this time, I'm making my way to what I really want, and no matter what others say, I know this is the right for me
